prompt: It is 2026. kolm.ai sells the best-in-class AI-agent security attestation: a vendor uploads agent logs and gets an Ed25519-signed, offline-verifiable evidence report with a scored red-team battery, mapped to SOC2/ISO42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP LLM/MITRE ATLAS, plus a transparency log, multi-framework export (Drata/Vanta/Excel), continuous re-attestation, and named co-signer tiers. To be the UNDISPUTED best in the world with NO further iterations needed, what is the complete state-of-the-art feature/capability set such a product MUST have in 2026? Enumerate exhaustively (product, cryptography, integrations, continuous monitoring, agent-identity, reporting/UX, buyer trust) and for each say whether it is table-stakes or a differentiator. Cite the latest standards/products (with URLs/@handle+date). End with the specific gaps kolm most likely still has versus this ultimate spec.

What are the 2026 emerging STANDARDS for AI AGENT IDENTITY, authorization, and provenance that a security-attestation product must support to be SOTA: NIST AI agent / agentic AI guidance, SPIFFE/SPIRE for workload identity, OAuth 2.1 + token-bound agent credentials, the IETF/OpenID work on agent auth, MCP (Model Context Protocol) security, Anthropic/OpenAI agent identity schemes, W3C verifiable credentials for agents, and any 'agent passport' / 'agent attestation' specs. For each: what it specifies, adoption status, and what an attestation report should capture about an agent's identity + scopes + delegation. Cite primary sources (URLs, @handle+date). Conclude with exactly what kolm should add to its report to be the agent-identity SOTA.

2026 state of the art for VERIFIABLE cryptographic evidence that a sophisticated security auditor would demand of a signed attestation product: RFC 3161 / RFC 5816 trusted timestamping, a PUBLIC append-only transparency log with Merkle inclusion proofs + signed tree heads (Sigstore Rekor / Trillian / RFC 6962 + 9162 + the C2SC/witness cosigning model), key rotation + revocation + a documented trust root, post-quantum signatures (ML-DSA/SLH-DSA migration), reproducible/deterministic builds, COSE/JWS envelope standards, and in-toto/SLSA provenance. kolm currently: Ed25519 signature, two-tier issuer-keyring provenance, an internal Merkle transparency log (not yet public/witnessed), offline browser verify. Identify precisely which of these SOTA properties kolm lacks and the order to add them to be cryptographically best-in-class. Cite standards/projects.

Latest 2026 state of every competitor/adjacent player to kolm (signed AI-agent security attestation): Cyphrex, Vanta (Agentic Trust), Drata, Lakera, Protect AI, HiddenLayer, Robust Intelligence (Cisco), Calypso AI, plus any new entrant doing SIGNED agent evidence. For each: their current best feature, pricing, and the ONE thing they do that kolm should match or beat. Quote the most recent announcements (@handle+date / URLs). Then: what is the single most defensible wedge kolm can own that none of them can easily copy, and what 3 capabilities would make kolm clearly #1 in 'signed, verifiable, deal-closing agent security evidence'. Be current and specific.

For a SOTA self-serve B2B security-evidence product in 2026, map the IDEAL end-to-end logistics/workflow for BOTH sides: (a) the AI vendor (seller) journey - discover -> free scan -> understand findings -> fix -> buy signed report -> hand to buyer -> continuous; what onboarding, log-ingestion (SDK/CI/connectors/sidecar), remediation guidance, and dashboard moments must exist; (b) the enterprise buyer (reviewer) journey - receive the Trust link -> verify -> map to their questionnaire -> approve; what makes a reviewer trust + reuse it. Identify the highest-friction steps that kill conversion and the SOTA way each is solved (cite real products). Conclude with the workflow gaps kolm should close to make both journeys frictionless.

2026 SEO + content-authority playbook for a technical B2B security product targeting 'AI agent security', 'AI agent security audit', 'signed security attestation', 'SOC 2 for AI agents', 'EU AI Act agent compliance', and procurement/security-review buyers. What technical SEO (Core Web Vitals, structured data/JSON-LD types: SoftwareApplication/Service/FAQ/HowTo, sitemap, canonical, metadata, OG), content (pillar pages, comparison pages, framework guides, the buyer's-questionnaire content, glossary), and authority signals (backlinks, G2/review sites, docs) actually move rankings + conversion for this category now? Which keywords have real intent + low competition for a new entrant. Cite specifics. Conclude with a prioritized SEO + content action list kolm should ship.
